title: Add session ID to protocol for multiplayer readiness (DES-025)
M9: LLM DM (dm mode)
description: LLMInterpreter + LLMNarrator via Claude MCP tools. crypt dm subcommand. FakeLLMInterpreter/Narrator used in CI. Architecture validation: LLM in the loop before heavy engine investment.

title: Context injection: pass game state in SLM user message
description: Inject room, items, exits, enemies, equipment, and spells into the user message sent to the SLM. Collapses the output space from open-ended to a small enumeration. ~100-200 extra tokens per request. Expected to fix item resolution and verb confusion failures. See docs/slm-improvement.md Lever 1.
close_reason: Rules-first routing with SLM ID resolution. Context injection. Eval accuracy 63.5% → 98.4%.

title: M14: Biff Multi-player
description: Party play. Each Biff participant controls one character. GameState.Party promoted from single-element to real party. move/attack/flee/defend character_id routing active. Turn tokens via Biff /write. Shared save state across sessions. DM narrates for full party.

title: GameState.Party: support real multi-character party
description: Party promoted from single-element to real party. move/attack/flee/defend all accept character_id routing (DES-021 pre-wired from M1).

title: Shared save state across Biff sessions
Unify toolRegistry and dispatch switch into single tool registration
description: Replace the decoupled toolRegistry var and dispatch switch with a single registration pattern where each tool registers both its schema and handler function.

title: Scenario topology validator and auto-fixer
description: Build a Go tool (or extend cryptd validate) that checks scenario YAML for: (1) one-way connections (room A→B exists but B→A doesn't), (2) rooms with >6 connections needed (6-direction limit), (3) unreachable rooms from starting_room, (4) rooms where all exits lead to full parents. Auto-suggest fixes: add reciprocal connections, insert hub rooms to split overloaded parents, report truly unreachable rooms. This is the 'Topology' phase from M11.5 (cryptd-2z6). Motivated by PR #45 where 200 hand-authored rooms had 83 broken one-way connections.

title: M11.5: Scenario Generation Pipeline (DES-025)
description: Four-phase scenario generation: Topology → Geography → Population → Narrative. Phase 1 (Topology) is the most urgent — PR #45 proved that hand-authoring 200 rooms produces 83 broken one-way connections without validation tooling. The 6-direction grid constraint means a filesystem tree doesn't map directly; hub rooms and topology validation are required infrastructure, not premature optimization. See cryptd-2pr for the validator task.

title: M7: save/load in-game
description: crypt save/load/list subcommands. Multiple save slots. Auto-save on room transition.

title: cryptd serve --passthrough flag for MCP/Claude Code mode
description: Add --passthrough flag to cryptd serve. Default is normal mode (interpreter + narrator, display-ready text). Passthrough skips interpreter and narrator, returns structured JSON via MCP tool surface. This is the mode the crypt Claude Code plugin connects to.
close_reason: Normal mode (play handler + interpreter/narrator wiring) and --passthrough flag implemented and tested

title: M1: Data Contracts
description: model structs, dice parser, scenario YAML parser+validator, save/load, crypt validate subcommand, test fixtures. Completed in commit 0bf30e5. Coverage: dice 100%, scenario 97.4%, save 94.4%.

title: internal/model: all GameState structs
description: GameState, Character, Stats, Equipment, Condition, Item, DungeonState, RoomState, LogEntry. Zero methods — behaviour lives in engine packages. DES-021 party-ready: Party is always []Character.

title: internal/dice: notation parser + tests
description: Parse('2d6+3') → {Count:2, Sides:6, Modifier:3}. Roll(), Min(), Max(). ParseError{Notation, Reason}. 100% coverage achieved.

title: internal/scenario: types + YAML parser + validator + tests
description: Scenario, Room, Connection{Room,Type}, ScenarioItem, EnemyTemplate. Load(path)+Validate(s). Typed errors: MissingFieldError, BrokenRoomRefError, UnknownEnemyError, InvalidDiceError. 97.4% coverage.

title: testdata/scenarios/invalid/: 6 broken fixture files
description: missing-starting-room, broken-room-ref, missing-room-name, unknown-enemy-template, invalid-dice-enemy, invalid-dice-item. Each triggers a specific typed error.

title: internal/save: Save/Load + tests + fixture
description: Save()/Load() for .dungeon/saves/<slot>.json. SchemaVersion=1.0. ErrVersionMismatch. Unknown fields silently ignored. testdata/saves/fighter-level-3.json. 94.4% coverage.

title: cmd/crypt: validate subcommand
description: crypt validate <file> — loads and validates scenario YAML. Prints OK exits 0 on success, error message exits 1 on failure. Wires internal/scenario.Load + Validate.

title: M8b-4: Basic text renderer for cryptd serve -t
description: Strip internal/renderer/cli.go down to a basic text renderer suitable for -t testing mode and scripted demos. Plain text output — room name, narration, simple HP numbers. No HP bars, no readline (stdin scanner only). The fancy display code (formatBar, formatHUD, formatEnemyLine) is removed from this package — it moves to the client in M8b-3. The autoplay renderer stays as-is (it's already minimal).

title: Add Response constructor functions to enforce Result/Error mutual exclusivity
description: Add successResponse/errorResponse constructors for the Response type in protocol.go to enforce the JSON-RPC mutual exclusivity invariant (exactly one of Result/Error).

title: SLM Interpreter Accuracy: 63% → 90%
description: Systematic improvement of SLM interpreter accuracy from 63.5% (gemma3:1b baseline) to 90%+. Four levers in priority order: context injection, prompt engineering, fine-tuning (SFT then RLEF), bigger model (last resort). Strategy doc: docs/slm-improvement.md. Eval harness: make eval-slm (63 labeled test cases, 80% pass threshold).
close_reason: Target was 90%, achieved 100% (63/63). Levers 1-2 (context injection + prompt engineering) were sufficient — fine-tuning and bigger model not needed.

title: M7: Full SLM + Medium Tier
description: Expand SLMInterpreter and SLMNarrator to cover all game verbs. Add medium tier (ollama Gemma 3 1B / Llama 3.2 3B). --model flag for explicit model selection. Eval harness for verb classification accuracy. solo mode declared feature-complete.
close_reason: M7 complete — all 5 tasks done (4 pre-existing, 1 new PR)

title: SLMInterpreter: expand system prompt and action schema to all verbs
description: Currently SLMInterpreter handles movement only. Expand to: attack, defend, flee, pick up, drop, equip, unequip, cast, examine, search, rest, save, load, inventory, help. Update system prompt and JSON action schema.
close_reason: Already implemented — SLMInterpreter SystemPrompt and validTypes cover all engine-supported verbs. Tests exist for all action types.

title: SLMNarrator: expand templates to all event types
description: Currently SLMNarrator handles movement narration only. Expand to: combat events, item events, leveling, conditions, spells, death, victory. Each event type gets a prompt template that produces atmospheric narration.
close_reason: Already implemented — SLMNarrator handles 7 atmospheric event types via SLM, delegates tactical/error events to template narrator. Full test coverage exists.

title: Medium tier defaults: ollama Gemma 3 1B / Llama 3.2 3B
description: Add medium tier to the inference probe sequence. Prefer Gemma 3 1B, fall back to Llama 3.2 3B. Medium tier produces richer narration than SmolLM2-135M. Update DefaultEndpoints() and probe logic.
close_reason: Merged PR #32 — model preferences in probe

title: --model flag for explicit model selection
description: Add --model flag to cryptd solo. Allows user to pick model explicitly (e.g. --model smollm2:135m, --model gemma3:1b). Runtime (llama.cpp vs ollama) auto-detected. Falls back gracefully if specified model not found.
close_reason: Already implemented — --model flag exists in cryptd solo, resolveRuntime handles explicit model + auto-detection fallback.

title: Eval harness: cmd/eval-slm verb classification accuracy
description: cmd/eval-slm runs the full verb table through each tier's default model and scores classification accuracy. Not CI — manual eval. Covers SmolLM2-135M (small), Gemma 3 1B (medium), Llama 3.2 3B (medium alt).
close_reason: Already implemented — cmd/eval-slm has 63 test cases, auto-detection, --model flag. Run with --model gemma3:1b or --model llama3.2:3b for medium tier eval.

title: Add validate() methods to dispatcher argument structs
description: Add validate() *RPCError methods to dispatcher argument structs (newGameArgs, moveArgs, etc.) to move validation adjacent to type definitions.

title: Daemon normal mode: wire interpreter + narrator into dispatcher
description: The dispatcher currently calls the engine directly and returns structured JSON (passthrough mode). Add normal mode: accept raw text input, run through interpreter (SLM with Rules fallback), feed EngineAction to engine, run EngineEvent through narrator (SLM with Template fallback), return display-ready text. Reuse existing code: internal/interpreter/slm.go, internal/interpreter/rules.go, internal/narrator/slm.go, internal/narrator/template.go, internal/inference/client.go. Server auto-detects ollama and degrades gracefully.
close_reason: Normal mode (play handler + interpreter/narrator wiring) and --passthrough flag implemented and tested

title: Split crypt client binary from cryptd (cmd/crypt-client/ entry point)
close_reason: Implemented in feat/binary-split: two-binary split (cmd/cryptd server, cmd/crypt client) with crypt connect auto-start

title: M11: Lux renderer
description: LuxRenderer calls lux.show() on scene transitions, lux.update() for incremental changes (HP ticks, narration appends). Never show() on every HP tick. FakeLuxServer integration test guards against regression.

title: M2: Thin End-to-End Slice
description: A player can type 'cryptd headless --scenario <id>' and move between two rooms. The full pipeline (interpreter → engine → narrator → renderer) exists and is wired. FIRST FULL ARCHITECTURE VALIDATION. Done when: cryptd headless --scenario minimal runs. CI green.

title: internal/engine: move, look, new_game
description: move(direction) → updates CurrentRoom, appends log entry, returns MoveResult{NewRoom,Exits,Items,Enemies}. look() → returns current room description. new_game(scenario, character) → initialises GameState. Unit tests: open door, locked door (error), unknown direction, fog-of-war update.

title: internal/interpreter/rules.go: RulesInterpreter
description: Recognises: go <dir>, n/s/e/w, look, quit. Returns unknown action for everything else (not an error). Unit tests: full verb table ≥20 cases.

title: internal/narrator/template.go: TemplateNarrator
description: Handles: moved, looked, unknown_action. One-sentence templates; no creativity. Unit tests: each event type produces non-empty string.

title: internal/renderer/cli.go: CLIRenderer
description: Prints room name, exits list, narration text to stdout. Reads one line from stdin, returns as InputEvent. Events() returns channel.

title: cmd/crypt: headless subcommand
description: cryptd headless --scenario <id> starts interactive game loop. Uses RulesInterpreter + TemplateNarrator + CLIRenderer wired through game.Loop. CRYPT_SCENARIO_DIR env var controls scenario lookup path.

title: testdata/scripts/minimal-run.yaml: E2E script
description: testdata/scripts/minimal-run.yaml: acceptance script consumed by headless E2E test. Documents the scenario used by 'cryptd headless --scenario minimal'.

title: Integration and E2E tests for M2 pipeline
description: Integration: new_game → move(S) → look → move(N) wires all three interfaces, asserts GameState after each step. E2E: crypt headless --script minimal-run.yaml exits 0. All engine packages ≥90% coverage.

title: Define RPCErrorCode named type for JSON-RPC error codes
description: Define type RPCErrorCode int in protocol.go instead of bare int for the Code field, improving discoverability and preventing accidental use of raw integers.

title: Replace dispatcher map[string]any responses with named structs
description: Replace map[string]any response bodies in dispatcher.go with named response structs (e.g., lookResponse, heroResponse, moveResponse) for compile-time safety and key typo detection.

title: eval-balance: monkey test harness for game balance tuning
close_reason: Committed to PR #47: eval-balance monkey test harness

title: M10: Daemon Session Routing
description: Daemon supports multiple concurrent sessions, DM-privileged tool routing, tools/list_changed push. Pre-requisite for multi-player and production-quality DM mode. Session isolation: game A state cannot bleed into game B.

title: Session identity injection
description: From mcp-proxy, or via --session-id flag until mcp-proxy ships.

title: DM vs player tool privilege gating
description: DM-only tools return typed error when called from player identity.

title: tools/list_changed push after player actions
description: Push tools/list_changed to DM client only after any player action.

title: Session isolation: game A state cannot bleed into game B
Multi-session integration tests
description: Two in-process fake clients: DM identity vs player identity. DM-only tool from player → typed error. Player action → tools/list_changed received by DM only. Two concurrent sessions; mutation in A does not affect B.

title: Move autoplay to cryptd (test harness, not a client mode)
description: autoplay is a testing tool that feeds a script file through the server. It belongs in cmd/cryptd/ as cryptd autoplay, not in the client. Remove from cmd/crypt/.
close_reason: Thin client implemented, autoplay/headless moved to cryptd

title: M8b-3: Client RPC Renderer with fancy ASCII display
description: Implement the client-side RPC Renderer in cmd/crypt. Receives typed PlayResponse over JSON-RPC, deserializes into model.GameState + narration, and renders with fancy ASCII display: HP/MP bars with block characters (formatBar/formatHUD), room headers, enemy status during combat. Uses readline for input, serializes as JSON-RPC play requests. cmd/crypt imports internal/model for typed data contracts. Replaces the current displayResult/printHeroStatus/map[string]any deserialization.

title: M8b-1: Define typed wire protocol for play responses
description: Define typed structs for the play request/response wire protocol. Replace the ad-hoc map[string]any construction in the play handler with a proper PlayResponse struct containing model.GameState, narration string, and terminal flags (dead, quit). This is the contract both renderer twins serialize/deserialize. Lives in internal/daemon/protocol.go alongside existing JSON-RPC types.

title: M10: daemon and crypt serve
description: crypt serve: engine as daemon on Unix domain socket. NDJSON protocol. net.Listen(unix). mcp-proxy shim per session. Race-safe concurrent MCP connections.

title: System prompt engineering: aliases, few-shot examples, edge cases
description: Add alias table (n=north, l=look, i=inventory, a=attack, ?=help, q=quit), 5-10 few-shot examples covering failure categories, reinforced snake_case rules, and explicit meta command handling. Expected to fix ~14 cases (single-char aliases, meta commands, ID normalization). See docs/slm-improvement.md Lever 2.
close_reason: System prompt with few-shot examples, stronger unknown guidance, item ID resolution instructions. Eval: 98.4% → 100%. Also added descend/ascend synonyms to rules.

title: M8: SLM narrator (solo mode)
description: SLMNarrator POSTs to ollama http://localhost:11434/api/generate. Default model phi3. 10s timeout. Falls back to TemplateNarrator if ollama unavailable. SLMInterpreter with 5s timeout, fallback to RulesInterpreter.

title: M12: scenario editor and validation CLI
description: crypt validate extended: warn on description_seed missing, check item/enemy balance. crypt new-scenario scaffold command.

title: M9: DM Thin Slice
description: crypt dm works. A Claude Code session can connect via MCP, start a game, and receive narration from the LLM. Thin slice: movement and basic narration only. FIRST FULL DM MODE VALIDATION. Done when: crypt dm invoked by /crypt skill starts a playable game with Claude narrating room descriptions.

title: internal/interpreter/llm.go: LLMInterpreter thin
description: Makes MCP callback to Claude with player input + room context. Returns structured action JSON. Thin slice: movement only.

title: internal/narrator/llm.go: LLMNarrator thin
description: Calls Claude with event result + adventure context. Returns narration string. Thin slice: movement and room descriptions.

title: skills/crypt/SKILL.md: DM role rewrite
description: Rewritten for DM role: narration instructions, not game rules. Handles room descriptions, item examine responses. No game logic in the LLM prompt (red line).
close_reason: SKILL.md lives in the crypt plugin repo, not cryptd. Not a cryptd bead.

title: cmd/crypt/dm.go: crypt dm subcommand
close_reason: crypt dm subcommand does not exist. crypt is a thin client — no subcommands. Superseded by DES-025 revision.

title: DM pipeline integration tests (FakeLLMInterpreter + FakeLLMNarrator)
description: Full DM mode pipeline with fakes — engine transitions identical to headless, only Interpreter and Narrator differ. LLM never called in tests. Fakes assert correct prompt structure is assembled.

title: M8: Daemon Thin Slice
description: crypt serve starts and handles a single MCP client connection. Full daemon topology exercised for the first time. Done when: MCP wire smoke tests pass; daemon can handle a complete game session from a single client.

title: cmd/crypt/serve.go: crypt serve subcommand + Unix socket daemon
description: Starts daemon, listens on Unix domain socket (net.Listen unix). Accepts one MCP client connection. Dispatches all MCP tools to embedded engine. Responds with same JSON as embedded mode.
close_reason: Implemented: protocol.go, dispatcher.go, handler.go, daemon.go, serve.go, main.go updated

title: Auto-start server from crypt connect (fork cryptd serve if socket not present)
description: If socket not present, crypt dm starts the daemon automatically.
close_reason: Implemented in feat/binary-split: two-binary split (cmd/cryptd server, cmd/crypt client) with crypt connect auto-start

title: Daemon integration and E2E tests
description: Integration (in-process): spawn daemon, connect one fake MCP client, call new_game + move + save_game, verify JSON responses. E2E: spawn crypt serve subprocess, connect minimal MCP client over stdio, call each tool once, assert valid JSON no stderr.
close_reason: Implemented: 17 unit tests, 3 integration tests, 1 E2E test — all passing with -race

title: crypt-admin: graph-first scenario generation (DES-027)
close_reason: PR #47 opened: crypt-admin binary with graph-first scenario generation

title: M11: Full DM Mode
description: DM mode fully featured. LLMInterpreter and LLMNarrator cover all game actions. /crypt:create skill: DM generates scenario YAML interactively. DM can generate encounter backstories and puzzle hints on demand. crypt dm declared feature-complete.

title: /crypt:create skill: DM generates scenario YAML interactively
DM encounter backstories and puzzle hints on demand
M7: Full SLM (solo mode complete)
description: SLMInterpreter and SLMNarrator cover all game actions across both small (llama.cpp + SmolLM2) and medium (ollama + Gemma/Llama) tiers. --model flag for user model selection. Solo mode fully playable as first-class experience. Includes model eval harness cmd/eval-slm (manual, not CI). crypt solo declared feature-complete.

title: SLMInterpreter expanded: all verbs and action schema
close_reason: Already complete — SLM interpreter was built with full verb coverage in M6 (PR #20). All 17 action types in system prompt, validTypes, validateFields, and test suite.

title: SLMNarrator expanded: all event types (combat, items, leveling)
cmd/eval-slm: model eval harness
description: Runs full verb table through real ollama model and scores classification accuracy. Not CI — run manually.

title: --model flag and runtime auto-detection
description: Add --model flag to crypt solo and crypt dm. Runtime auto-detection: probe ollama (HTTP health check), probe llama-server (binary exists + can spawn), fall back to regex. User-specified model used with whichever runtime is available.

title: Make RPCError implement the error interface
description: Add Error() string method to RPCError so it implements the error interface and can be used with standard Go error handling idioms.

title: M8b-2: Server RPC Renderer
description: Implement a Server RPC Renderer in internal/daemon that satisfies model.Renderer. Render() serializes GameState + narration as a typed PlayResponse JSON-RPC response and writes it to the connection. Events() reads JSON-RPC play requests from the connection and feeds them into an InputEvent channel. This renderer replaces the ad-hoc play handler — the game loop calls Loop.Run() with this renderer instead of manually calling Dispatch(). The renderer handles the initialize/new_game handshake before entering the render-wait loop.

title: M4: Lux Thin Slice
description: crypt solo starts, connects to Lux, shows minimal HUD. Player can move rooms and see narration log update. No SLM yet — solo still uses RulesInterpreter. Done when: crypt solo --scenario minimal connects to running Lux; FakeLuxServer tests pass in CI without Lux installed.

title: internal/renderer/lux.go: LuxRenderer thin
description: show() on new_game and room transitions: displays room name and narration in single Lux text panel. update() for log appends. recv() event loop for Lux button presses → InputEvent. Never call show() for incremental updates (performance red line guarded by FakeLuxServer test).

title: cmd/crypt/solo.go: crypt solo subcommand
description: Wires RulesInterpreter + TemplateNarrator + LuxRenderer. Falls back to CLIRenderer if Lux is not running.

title: FakeLuxServer integration tests for LuxRenderer
description: show() called on scene transition, not update() (regression guard). update() called for log appends not full show(). FakeLuxServer receives correct element structure for two-room navigation. FakeLuxServer injects synthetic button press; assert engine receives correct InputEvent.

title: M5: leveling and XP
description: XP awards on enemy defeat, level thresholds, stat gain on level-up. Per-class tables matching Wizardry I.

title: M8b-5: Wire up twin renderers, remove old play handler
description: Integration task: wire the Server RPC Renderer into the daemon's connection handler so Loop.Run() drives it directly. Remove the ad-hoc play handler (internal/daemon/play.go), heroSummary(), and the exported Loop.Dispatch(). The daemon creates the RPC renderer per connection, runs the game loop with it, and the loop's natural render-wait cycle maps to JSON-RPC request-response. Update the passthrough mode path to coexist (passthrough still uses the dispatcher directly for MCP tool calls). Update all daemon and integration tests to use typed PlayResponse instead of map[string]any.

title: M14: release polish
description: MCP schema contract test (dump-mcp-schema diff). README install instructions. Version flag. Release CI job.

title: Fine-tuning: SFT on eval cases, then RLEF with engine reward
description: Phase A: supervised fine-tuning on 500-1000 labeled pairs (63 eval cases + synthetic expansions). Phase B: reinforcement learning using the deterministic engine as reward signal — sample completions, run through engine, prefer accepted actions. Target: domain-specialized 1B model at 95%+ accuracy. See docs/slm-improvement.md Lever 3.
close_reason: Not needed — achieved 100% accuracy with context injection + prompt engineering alone.

title: M8b: Twin Renderer — Renderer interface across the wire (DES-026)
description: Implement the twin renderer pattern (DES-026): the Renderer interface spans the client-server boundary as a matched pair. Server RPC Renderer serializes GameState + narration as JSON-RPC responses and deserializes play requests into InputEvents. Client RPC Renderer deserializes responses into typed model.GameState, displays fancy ASCII (HP/MP bars, room headers, enemy status), and serializes readline input as JSON-RPC requests. Eliminates map[string]any from play response paths. Replaces ad-hoc play handler and exported Loop.Dispatch() with the RPC Renderer wired into Loop.Run(). cmd/crypt/ imports internal/model for typed data contracts. See DESIGN.md DES-026 and docs/build-plan.md M8b.

title: M12: Lux HUD Release Pass
description: 2-3 candidate UI designs for first release. Map canvas with grid coords from M11.5. Explore Wizardry I reference material. Candidate selection via playtesting. Visual polish, theme support, multiple UI support.

title: Thin client: delete embedded.go, crypt is a dumb pipe
description: Delete cmd/crypt/embedded.go. Rewrite crypt as a single thin client that connects to cryptd serve, sends text, displays text. No engine imports. No parseCommand(), no formatToolResult(), no SLM. Forks cryptd serve via dialOrStart() if not running. The client binary must not transitively import internal/engine, internal/game, internal/interpreter, internal/narrator, internal/renderer, internal/scenario, internal/save, or internal/dice.
close_reason: Thin client implemented, autoplay/headless moved to cryptd

title: M6: fog-of-war and map
description: visited_rooms tracking, fog reveal on entry, map export for CLIRenderer. RoomState.Cleared flag.

title: M3: combat engine
description: Turn-based combat loop: initiative, attack rolls, damage (dice), flee/defend. Combat state machine in internal/engine/combat/. ≥90% coverage.

title: Remove PlayMode="dm" from dispatcher — server is mode-agnostic (DES-025)
close_reason: Removed PlayMode=dm from dispatchNewGame; server is mode-agnostic per DES-025

title: M3: Full Engine and Headless Mode
description: Deepen engine to cover all game mechanics. headless is a complete playable mode. Acceptance test suite runs for the first time. Done when: all 5 acceptance scripts pass, engine coverage ≥90%, a real human can play a complete game via crypt headless.
close_reason: M3 complete: combat, spells, leveling, save/load, help, HUD, MCP schema contract, 5 acceptance scripts. PRs #8-#15 merged.

title: Character and inventory: pick_up, drop, equip, use_item, examine
description: Weight limits, slot conflicts. RulesInterpreter verbs: pick up, drop, equip, unequip, examine. Unit tests first.

title: 5 acceptance scripts passing for the first time
description: YAML game scripts in testdata/scripts/ and e2e/acceptance/. Scenarios: full run, combat, pick up item, level up, save and reload. All pass via crypt headless --script.

title: Combat engine: initiative, attack, defend, flee, conditions, AI
description: Initiative order, attack/defend/flee/end_combat actions. All AI patterns (aggressive/cautious/scripted). Conditions (poisoned/asleep/paralyzed/confused). Unit tests: combat sequence new_game→move→attack×N→end_combat→verify XP.

description: save_game and load_game as engine MCP tools. Test: mid-combat save → load → assert state identical.

description: Handles: combat_start, combat_end, item_picked_up, item_dropped, equipped, spell_cast, level_up, died, save_ok, load_ok, unknown_action. All produce non-empty strings.

Map canvas: draw element, grid rooms, fog of war, player dot

description: go module, testutil fakes (FakeLLMInterpreter, FakeLLMNarrator, FakeSLMServer, FakeLuxServer, ScriptRunner), CI workflows, testdata fixtures, markdownlint config. Completed in commit 3c6e3bc.

title: Go module scaffold, directory layout, build tags
description: go.mod (github.com/punt-labs/cryptd), directory layout: cmd/crypt, cmd/dump-mcp-schema, internal/model, internal/testutil, testdata/. Build tags: integration, e2e, acceptance. Unit tests have no tag.

title: CI workflow: test.yml
description: 5 CI jobs: unit (go test -race ./...), integration (-tags integration), coverage gate (≥90% engine), lint (go vet + staticcheck), markdownlint.

title: testutil fakes: FakeLLMInterpreter, FakeLLMNarrator, FakeSLMServer, FakeLuxServer, ScriptRunner
description: All in internal/testutil/. FakeSLMServer uses httptest.NewServer. FakeLuxServer records show()/update() calls and injects events via channel. ScriptRunner skeleton for YAML scripts.

title: testdata layout: scenarios/, saves/, scripts/, fixtures/
description: testdata/scenarios/minimal.yaml — two rooms, one enemy, one item. testdata/fixtures/ollama-*.json canned responses.

title: Markdownlint config and CI integration
description: .markdownlint.jsonc + .markdownlint-cli2.jsonc. Rules disabled to match existing doc style. Session export files excluded. npx markdownlint-cli2 in CI.

title: Add TCP listener support to cryptd serve (--listen flag) alongside Unix socket
close_reason: TCP support added: NewTCPServer constructor, --listen flag in serve.go, public Serve(ln) method, TCP integration tests

title: M13: Rich World
description: Multiple bundled scenarios; advanced mechanics. Shops (buy/sell, gold economy), traps (DEX save on room entry), cursed items + identify spell, minimum three bundled scenarios, permadeath mode configurable per scenario.

title: Traps: DEX save on room entry
Cursed items: equip without knowing, identify spell to reveal
Three bundled scenarios
description: Minimum three complete scenario YAML files beyond minimal.yaml.

title: cryptd serve should daemonize by default (fork, detach, PID file)
description: cryptd serve currently runs in the foreground like -f is always on. Real daemons (sshd -D, named -f) fork to background by default. Three modes: (1) cryptd serve — daemonize, listen on socket, PID file. (2) cryptd serve -f — foreground, still listen on socket (debugging/containers/supervisors). (3) cryptd serve -t — testing mode, stdin/stdout, no socket, implies -f. This replaces the separate headless/autoplay subcommands. -t is what headless was, correctly named as a flag on serve. autoplay is just piping a script into crypt connected to cryptd serve -t.
close_reason: Implemented and tested: cryptd serve daemonizes by default, -f foreground, -t testing mode. All 8 e2e tests pass.

title: M6: Small Tier Thin Slice (DES-023)
description: SLMInterpreter + SLMNarrator with llama.cpp sidecar (SmolLM2-135M) for the small tier. Thin slice handles only movement commands. Four-tier failover: ollama → llama.cpp → regex+templates (DES-023). Done when: crypt solo auto-detects llama.cpp or ollama, uses SLM for movement narration, falls back gracefully; all SLM tests pass in CI without any model runtime.

title: internal/interpreter/slm.go: SLMInterpreter thin
description: SLMInterpreter thin slice: HTTP client for llama.cpp-compatible /v1/chat/completions endpoint. Compact system prompt maps free-text to structured actions. 5s timeout, fallback to RulesInterpreter. Works with both llama.cpp sidecar and ollama.

title: internal/narrator/slm.go: SLMNarrator thin
description: SLMNarrator thin slice: HTTP client for llama.cpp-compatible /v1/chat/completions endpoint. Expands description_seed into 2-4 sentences. 10s timeout, fallback to TemplateNarrator. Works with both llama.cpp sidecar and ollama.

title: SLM fallback and error path tests (FakeSLMServer)
description: Four-tier failover tests with FakeSLMServer: happy path, timeout→fallback, malformed JSON→fallback, non-200→fallback, runtime probe order (ollama→llama.cpp→regex). llama.cpp sidecar lifecycle (spawn, health check, shutdown).

title: Runtime auto-detection: discover running ollama/llama.cpp servers
description: Process management for llama.cpp sidecar in the small tier. Spawn llama-server with the bundled SmolLM2-135M GGUF, wait for health endpoint, proxy requests, shutdown on context cancellation. Probe logic: check ollama first, then llama.cpp, then fall back to regex.

title: internal/inference/: OpenAI-compatible HTTP client
description: Shared HTTP client calling /v1/chat/completions. Works with both llama.cpp and ollama. Configurable base URL, model name, timeout. Structured JSON response parsing with retry on malformed output. This is the foundation both SLMInterpreter and SLMNarrator use.
